addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

You are an agent working on the **bim-ai** repo (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).

---

## Repo orientation

```
packages/core/src/index.ts                                   — Element union + ElemKind
packages/web/src/osm/project.ts                              — project settings Zustand store
packages/web/src/workspace/Workspace.tsx                     — app shell (where dialogs mount)
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectSetupDialog.tsx    — project setup (see phase wiring)
packages/web/src/phases/PhaseManagerDialog.tsx               — pattern for a settings dialog
packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts                       — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts                        — per-tool grammar state machines
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts                    — 3D mesh dispatcher
packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts             — plan symbol dispatcher
```

Tests: co-located `*.test.ts` — run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.

---

## What wave 1 already built — DO NOT rebuild these

- `project_settings` element type exists in `@bim-ai/core` with `projectUnits`, `georeference`
- `PhaseManagerDialog.tsx` (574 lines) — fully wired in Workspace.tsx — use as pattern
- `DecalElem` element type exists in `@bim-ai/core` (search `kind: 'decal'`)
- `buildDecalMesh()` exists in `meshBuilders.ts` for 3D rendering of decals
- Column element type exists in `@bim-ai/core` with `heightMm`, `widthMm`, `positionMm`
- Column mesh builder exists in `meshBuilders.ts`

---

## Tasks

### F1 — Global parameters dialog (Ch. 3.8)

**F1a. Data model**: Add to `project_settings` in `core/index.ts`:
```ts
globalParams?: Array<{
  id: string;
  name: string;
  formula: string;   // e.g. "3000 + 500" or "2 * floorHeight" — stored as string
  valueMm: number;   // evaluated result, cached on save
}>;
```

**F1b. Commands**:
- `{ type: 'addGlobalParam', name: string, formula: string }`
- `{ type: 'updateGlobalParam', id: string, formula: string, valueMm: number }`
- `{ type: 'deleteGlobalParam', id: string }`

**F1c. Dialog**: Create `packages/web/src/workspace/project/GlobalParamsDialog.tsx`:
- Renders a table: columns "Name", "Formula", "Value (mm)"
- Each row is editable inline; formula field validates as a numeric expression
- "Add Parameter" button appends a new row
- "Delete" button on each row
- Simple formula evaluator: use `Function('return ' + formula)()` or a safe expression
  parser (avoid arbitrary code exec — strip non-numeric chars except +−*/() and spaces)
- On any change, dispatch the appropriate command + recompute `valueMm`

**F1d. Workspace wiring**: In `Workspace.tsx`, add `globalParamsOpen` state + menu entry
the same way `phaseManagerOpen` and `PhaseManagerDialog` are wired. Study lines ~1041
and ~4148–4194 in Workspace.tsx for the exact pattern.

Tests:
- addGlobalParam command stores the param in project_settings
- Formula "3000 + 500" evaluates to valueMm=3500
- Dialog renders a row for each globalParam

Update tracker §3.8: "Implemented — global params table + dialog + commands"

---

### F2 — Decal placement tool

**F2a. ToolId**: Add `'decal'` to `toolRegistry.ts` (hotkey `DC`, 3D mode only).

**F2b. Grammar**: Add `DecalState` / `reduceDecal` to `toolGrammar.ts`:
```
idle
  → 3D click on a face → picking-image (store position + face normal)
picking-image
  → image chosen (via file picker or URL input in options bar) → done
  → Escape → idle
```
Effect on done: `{ kind: 'createDecal', positionMm, normalVec, imageSrc, widthMm: 1000, heightMm: 1000 }`

**F2c. PlanCanvas / viewport wiring**: Wire `case 'decal':` in PlanCanvas.tsx (or in the
3D viewport event handler — `DecalElem` is a 3D-only tool). On the effect, dispatch:
`{ type: 'createElement', elem: { kind: 'decal', ...effect } }`

**F2d. Inspector for selected decal**: Image URL field, width (mm) + height (mm) number
inputs. Dispatches `{ type: 'updateElement', id, patch: { imageSrc, widthMm, heightMm } }`.

The 3D mesh builder (`buildDecalMesh`) already exists — just ensure it's dispatched from
`meshBuilders.ts` for `kind === 'decal'`.

Tests:
- Grammar: 3D face click produces createDecal effect with correct normalVec
- Inspector renders imageSrc, widthMm, heightMm fields

Update tracker §8.1.5: "Implemented — 'decal' ToolId + grammar + inspector"

---

### F3 — Sloped / inclined columns (Ch. 9.1.4)

**F3a. Data model**: Add to the column element type in `core/index.ts`:
```ts
topOffsetXMm?: number;   // horizontal X shift of column top from base (default 0)
topOffsetYMm?: number;   // horizontal Y shift of column top from base (default 0)
```

**F3b. 3D mesh**: In `meshBuilders.ts` (column case), when `topOffsetXMm` or `topOffsetYMm`
is non-zero:
- Compute the inclined axis vector: `(topOffsetXMm, topOffsetYMm, heightMm)` — normalized
- Extrude the column profile along this axis instead of straight up
- The base profile stays at `positionMm` (XY); the top profile is offset by
  `(topOffsetXMm, topOffsetYMm)` at height `heightMm`

**F3c. Plan symbol**: In the column plan symbol renderer, when column has non-zero offsets:
- Draw the base footprint solid (existing)
- Draw the top footprint dashed, shifted by `(topOffsetXMm, topOffsetYMm)` in plan space
- Draw a diagonal line connecting the two footprint centres

**F3d. Inspector**: Add "Top Offset X (mm)" and "Top Offset Y (mm)" number inputs to the
column inspector panel. Add a read-only "Inclination angle" field computed as:
`Math.atan2(Math.hypot(topOffsetXMm, topOffsetYMm), heightMm) * (180 / Math.PI)`.

Tests:
- Column with `topOffsetXMm: 500, heightMm: 3000`: verify top vertices in 3D mesh
  are shifted by 500mm in X relative to base vertices
- Zero offsets produces same output as before (no regression)

Update tracker §9.1.4: "Implemented — sloped column data model + mesh + plan symbol + inspector"

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before editing `core/index.ts`, `toolRegistry.ts`,

with open('packages/core/src/index.ts', 'r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()

# Find the Element union - starts at line 1120 (0-indexed: 1119)
# It's a multi-line type union. We need to find when the type assignment ends.
# Look for a line that is just a semicolon or empty at the right nesting level

depth = 0
in_element = False
start = 1119  # 0-indexed line 1120

for i, line in enumerate(lines[start:], start=start):
    stripped = line.strip()
    if i == start:
        in_element = True
    
    if not in_element:
        continue
    
    # Count braces
    depth += stripped.count('{') - stripped.count('}')
    
    # The union ends at the semicolon of the export type statement
    # When we're at depth 0 and see a line that's just a pipe (|) followed by something, it's still part
    # But when we see a line starting with 'export' or similar, the union may have ended
    if i > start and depth == 0 and stripped == ';':
        print(f"Union might end at line {i+1} (0-indexed: {i})")
        for j in range(max(0, i-3), min(len(lines), i+5)):
            print(f"  {j+1}: {lines[j]}", end='')
        break
    
    if i > start and depth <= 0 and stripped.startswith('export') and i > start + 10:
        print(f"New export at line {i+1}")
        for j in range(max(0, i-3), min(len(lines), i+3)):
            print(f"  {j+1}: {lines[j]}", end='')
        break
EOF

190	/**
191	 * Numeric-input field state — appears at the cursor while a numeric-capable
192	 * tool is mid-draw and the user types a digit. `axis` toggles on Tab so a
193	 * second number can drive the perpendicular direction.
194	 */
195	export interface NumericInputState {
196	  active: boolean;
197	  value: string;
198	  axis: 'primary' | 'perpendicular';
199	}
200	
201	export function initialNumericInputState(): NumericInputState {
202	  return { active: false, value: '', axis: 'primary' };
203	}
204	
205	export type NumericInputEvent =
206	  | { kind: 'start'; firstDigit: string }
207	  | { kind: 'append'; digit: string }
208	  | { kind: 'backspace' }
209	  | { kind: 'tab-axis' }
210	  | { kind: 'commit' }
211	  | { kind: 'cancel' };
212	
213	export function reduceNumericInput(
214	  state: NumericInputState,
215	  event: NumericInputEvent,
216	): NumericInputState {
217	  switch (event.kind) {
218	    case 'start':
219	      return { active: true, value: event.firstDigit, axis: 'primary' };
220	    case 'append':
221	      if (!state.active) return state;
222	      return { ...state, value: state.value + event.digit };
223	    case 'backspace':
224	      if (!state.active) return state;
225	      return { ...state, value: state.value.slice(0, -1) };
226	    case 'tab-axis':
227	      if (!state.active) return state;
228	      return {
229	        ...state,
230	        axis: state.axis === 'primary' ? 'perpendicular' : 'primary',
231	      };
232	    case 'commit':
233	    case 'cancel':
234	      return initialNumericInputState();
235	  }
236	}

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119
